title: facet model provides configurable classification dimensions
content:
# facet model provides configurable classification dimensions

`powerhouse/facet` is the simplest model — it defines classification dimensions (like "Region", "Industry", "Size") with selectable options. Other models reference facets for targeting and configuration.

## State Shape

```graphql
type FacetState {
    id: PHID!
    name: String!
    description: String
    lastModified: DateTime!
    options: [FacetOption!]!
}

type FacetOption {
    id: OID!
    label: String!
    description: String
    displayOrder: Int
    isDefault: Boolean!
}
```

## Modules (2)

| Module | Operations | Purpose |
|--------|-----------|---------|
| facet-management | 2 | Set facet name, description |
| option-management | 4 | Add/update/remove/reorder options |

## Referenced By

- ResourceTemplate.`facetBindings.facetType: PHID` → this model
- ServiceOffering.`facetBindings.facetType: PHID` → this model
- ResourceInstance.`configuration` uses facet categories
- SubscriptionInstance services have `facetSelections`

---

- [[schema design]]
- [[object relations]]
description: 6 operations across 2 modules — defines named facets with ordered options used for targeting and configuration across templates, offerings, and instances
